t: As Diplomatic Feud Deteriorates, Saudis Stop Buying Canadian Wheat, Barley
p: As Diplomatic Feud Deteriorates, Saudis Stop Buying Canadian Wheat, Barley Two days after an angry Saudi Arabia broke of diplomatic and trade relations with Canada over "blatant interference" in the country's domestic affairs, after Canada criticized Saudi Arabia’s human rights track records and slammed the arrests of women’s rights activists including Samar Badawi, a Canadian citizen, the feud has escalated and on Tuesday, Saudi Arabia’s main state wheat buying agency has told grains exporters it will no longer buy Canadian wheat and barley in its international tenders. Traders quoted by Reuters , said they had received an official notice from the Saudi Grains Organization (SAGO) about its decision.
